bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer is valid but gives me the lowest value on every issue, and their message is an emotional appeal without evidence of concession, so I will anchor firmly on my highest-preference values.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.973) because it is still very early (t=0.02) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueD offer. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the offer history shows they are holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD while keeping my important issues at valueA.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early, and the opponent has not conceded at all: all three opponent offers remain at valueD for every issue, which is low utility for me. Their messages are emotional appeals but do not change the bidding evidence, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession to keep movement going. The opponent has mostly held at very low-utility offers for me, with only a small concession on issueA, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding information. I will concede further on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.866: still strong for me, but with an additional concession on lower-weight issueC while keeping my most important issues A and B at valueA. Time is still very early, so there is no reason to move close to the opponent’s low-utility offer; however, their move from issueA:valueD to issueA:valueC suggests some willingness to concede despite the emotional messaging.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.814 because it is early at t=0.10, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only improved from all valueD to issueA:valueC while holding the other issues at valueD, so their actual concessions are limited despite cooperative/emotional messages. I will keep my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA, while conceding further on the lower-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.76, since time is still only 0.12 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s actual bids show limited concession only on issueA while holding issueB/issueC/issueD at valueD, so I will keep my high-weight issues A and B at valueA while conceding further on lower-weight issues C and D. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the bidding record is the reliable signal.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a relatively high utility while making a visible concession on lower-priority terms. The opponent has only improved issueA so far while holding issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueD, and their emotional messages are not binding; I will hold firm on the most valuable issueA while exploring a concession on issueB and issueD.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.733, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueC and issueD while holding firm on the most important issueA. At t=0.16 there is still limited time pressure, so I should not accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their bids improved briefly for me up to issueA:valueB but then worsened in the latest offer, so I treat their emotional partnership messages as cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68–0.70 because it is still early at t=0.18, but I should show some movement rather than hold at my ideal. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly insist on valueD for later issues, with only limited movement on issueA/issueB, so I will concede further on lower-weight issues while protecting issueA and issueB more strongly.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, which is still strong for me but gives full concessions on lower-weight issueC and issueD while holding firm on the more important issueA and issueB. At t=0.20 it is still early, so I should not rush to accept the opponent’s latest 0.52-utility offer, though their movement to issueA:valueA suggests issueA may now be settleable. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the bid history instead: they are conceding slowly, mainly on issueA, while holding the remaining issues at valueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.814 because it is still early at t=0.22, so there is no need to move down to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made some concessions on issueA and issueC but also regressed in the latest offer, while their messages are mainly emotional appeals rather than binding constraints. I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues A and B, while conceding on lower-weight issueD and partially on issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73, since it is early-mid negotiation and there is no need to concede down near the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made some movement on issueA and issueB, but their latest offer is still only about 0.39 utility to me, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while conceding on lower/medium issues to signal flexibility.

At t=0.26 it is still early enough to target a strong utility for me while continuing to concede mainly on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers are valid and show some movement on issueA/issueB, but they still keep issueC and issueD at my worst values; their messages are emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual bid history rather than the wording. I will hold firm on high-weight issueA, accept a moderate concession on issueB, and give ground on issueC/issueD without repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.733, since we are only at t=0.28 and should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, while the actual offers show limited and inconsistent movement; they have occasionally moved on issueA, issueB, or issueC, but mostly hold many issues at valueD. I will hold firm on my highest-weight issueA while conceding on lower-weight issues C and D and partially on B to keep the bid potentially workable.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because we are only at t=0.30 and the opponent’s offers to me remain quite low and inconsistent rather than steadily improving. I will continue conceding on the lowest-weight issueD and hold firm on issueA, while offering moderate movement on issueB and issueC based on their occasional willingness to move on those issues. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the actual bid history matters more than those appeals.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 while making a meaningful concession from my last offer by lowering issueC, since we are only about one-third through the negotiation and should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially recently on issueB, but their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual bid pattern rather than the wording.

At t=0.34 there is still time, so I am targeting a solid utility while making a visible concession on lower-weight issues rather than dropping to the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent’s bids have improved only intermittently for me, with their best signal being willingness to accept issueA: valueA once and some movement on issueB/issueC, while their emotional messages are not binding. I will hold firm on my highest-weight issueA, concede issueD fully, and offer compromise values on issueB and issueC without repeating my exact prior bid.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.68 while making meaningful concessions on lower-weight issues, since time is progressing but not yet near the deadline. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history: they have repeatedly pushed issueC and issueD toward valueD and have shown some movement on issueB, while issueA is my highest-weight issue and they have even offered valueA on it once.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding further on lower/mid-weight issues while holding issueA at my top value because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s offers remain quite low for me and their messages are mostly emotional appeals, but their bidding shows some movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so I am offering a tradeoff that improves terms on issueB/issueC/D without matching their low-value latest bid.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65 rather than making a large late-stage concession. The opponent’s offers have improved from all valueD to combinations like valueC/valueB, with the latest valid offer at about 0.443 utility to me, but their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding constraints. I will keep issueA at my strongest value because it is my highest-weight issue, while conceding on lower-weight issues to stay in the zone they have repeatedly signaled.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding mainly on lower/mid-weight issues while holding issueA at valueA because it is my most important issue. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat but remain low for me, and their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than reliable constraints, so I am making a constructive counter without dropping too far.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65 while still holding firm on issueA, my most important issue. The opponent’s offers have improved only slowly and remain low for me, but they have recently shown willingness around issueB/valueC and issueC/valueC, so I am conceding on lower-weight issues while keeping a strong core. Their messages are emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.60, making a meaningful mid-game concession without giving up the highest-weight issueA. The opponent’s offers have improved only inconsistently and their latest bid is still low for me, so I treat the emotional partnership messages as cheap talk and rely on the actual bid pattern; I concede further on lower/mid-weight issues while holding issueA firm.
